sentiment.rationale: The Lord's preface warns all the earth to repent, with judgment language throughout, tempered by promises of covenant restoration.
Glorious promise of Elijah's return to restore priesthood sealing power, though verse 3 warns of the earth being wasted.
Stern rebuke of Joseph Smith for fearing man more than God after the 116 pages were lost, with sorrow over transgression but ending in mercy.
An encouraging call to serve God with heart, might, mind, and strength; emphasizes faith, hope, charity, and the promise of harvest.
Warning to Martin Harris to humble himself, conditional promises about witnessing the plates, and threats of desolation if people reject the word.
Warm reassurance to Oliver Cowdery confirming his spiritual witness, granting gifts, and concluding with Christ's tender invitation to look unto Him.
Glorious vision of John the Beloved receiving power over death and being made a ministering angel to bring souls to Christ.
Peaceful teaching about the spirit of revelation—'I will tell you in your mind and in your heart, by the Holy Ghost'—with encouragement to exercise faith.
Correction of Oliver Cowdery for failing to study it out in his mind before asking; gentle rebuke mixed with patient encouragement.
Exposes Satan's plot against the lost 116 pages with strong warnings, but resolves with divine wisdom in the small plates solution and Christ's inclusive doctrine.
Gentle counsel to Hyrum Smith to wait and study before preaching, with promises of the Spirit enlightening his mind and filling his soul with joy.
Brief, encouraging call to Joseph Knight Sr. to participate in the restoration through humility, love, faith, and charity.
The majestic conferral of the Aaronic Priesthood by John the Baptist—a singular moment of priesthood restoration with keys of baptism and ministering of angels.
Encouraging call to David Whitmer promising eternal life through faithfulness, with the standard missionary harvest language.
Personal, intimate revelation telling John Whitmer the most valuable thing he can do is declare repentance and bring souls to Christ.
Virtually identical to Section 15, giving Peter Whitmer Jr. the same peaceful assurance that missionary work is the most valuable service.
The Lord promises the Three Witnesses a glorious view of the plates and sacred relics, declaring His grace sufficient for them.
Rich in the worth of souls and missionary joy, establishing repentance, baptism, and the calling of the Twelve Apostles—deeply comforting and purposeful.
Stern commands to repent with vivid description of Christ's suffering at every pore, warning Martin Harris of consequences, but offering peace through meekness.
Foundational church document establishing doctrines of the Godhead, baptism, sacrament ordinances, and priesthood duties—instructive and organizational in tone.

summaries.summary: The Lord introduces the Doctrine and Covenants as His preface, warning all inhabitants of the earth to repent and heed His servants' voices before His Second Coming.
The angel Moroni quotes a modified Malachi prophecy about Elijah returning to restore priesthood sealing power and turn the hearts of children to their fathers.
The Lord rebukes Joseph Smith for fearing man more than God after the 116 manuscript pages were lost, but assures him God's work cannot be frustrated.
A revelation to Joseph Smith Sr. calling him to serve God with all his heart, might, mind, and strength in the marvelous work of restoration.
The Lord promises three witnesses will see the gold plates, warns Martin Harris to humble himself, and foretells judgments upon the unbelieving.
The Lord confirms Oliver Cowdery's spiritual witness, grants him the gift of translation, and invites him to look unto Christ in every thought.
A translated parchment reveals that John the Beloved asked for and received power over death to remain on earth and bring souls to Christ.
The Lord teaches Oliver Cowdery about the spirit of revelation—knowledge given to mind and heart by the Holy Ghost—and grants him the gift of Aaron.
The Lord explains to Oliver Cowdery that translation requires studying it out in one's mind before asking God, and counsels patience.
The Lord reveals Satan's plot to discredit the Book of Mormon through the altered 116 pages and commands Joseph to translate the small plates of Nephi instead.
The Lord counsels Hyrum Smith to wait and study the scriptures before preaching, promising that the Spirit will enlighten his mind and fill his soul with joy.
A revelation to Joseph Knight Sr. calling him to the work of restoration, requiring humility, love, faith, and charity to assist.
John the Baptist confers the Aaronic Priesthood upon Joseph Smith and Oliver Cowdery, granting the keys of baptism, repentance, and ministering of angels.
The Lord calls David Whitmer to assist in the restoration, promising eternal life through faithfulness and declaring the fulness of the gospel.
The Lord tells John Whitmer that the most valuable thing he can do is declare repentance and bring souls unto Christ.
The Lord gives Peter Whitmer Jr. the same counsel as his brother John: the most valuable work is declaring repentance and bringing souls to Christ.
The Lord promises the Three Witnesses that through faith they will view the gold plates, breastplate, sword of Laban, Urim and Thummim, and Liahona.
The Lord declares the great worth of souls, establishes the doctrine of repentance and baptism, and commands Oliver Cowdery and David Whitmer to select the Twelve Apostles.
Jesus Christ reveals the meaning of eternal punishment, describes His agonizing suffering in Gethsemane, and commands Martin Harris to repent and fund the Book of Mormon printing.
The foundational articles of the Church establish doctrines of the Godhead, baptism, and sacrament, and define the duties of elders, priests, teachers, and deacons.

crossReferences.note: Both teach that the voice of God's servants is the same as His own voice.
Moroni quotes Malachi's prophecy about Elijah with slight modifications for the Restoration context.
Mormon's insertion of the small plates connects directly to the lost 116 pages crisis.
The list of virtues in verse 6 closely parallels Peter's ladder of Christian attributes.
Moroni's prophecy that three witnesses would see the plates is fulfilled through this revelation.
Christ identifies Himself using the same language as John's Gospel—'the light which shineth in darkness.'
Clarifies the New Testament account of Jesus's statement about John tarrying until He comes.
Moses parting the Red Sea is cited as an example of the spirit of revelation in action.
Builds on the previous revelation's teaching about receiving knowledge in mind and heart.
Both sections address the same crisis—the lost 116 manuscript pages—with complementary solutions.
Both passages define Christ's church as simply those who repent and come unto Him.
Later revelation confirms John the Baptist's role in restoring the Aaronic Priesthood.
The principle of establishing truth through multiple witnesses echoes Paul's teaching.
The Savior's parable of the lost sheep teaches the same principle about the worth of every soul.
King Benjamin's angel likewise prophesied that Christ would bleed at every pore from anguish.
Luke records Jesus sweating drops of blood in Gethsemane, paralleling this firsthand account.
The sacrament bread prayer is nearly identical to the one recorded in the Book of Mormon.
The sacrament water prayer matches the Book of Mormon ordinance language.

historicalContext.setting: Hiram, Ohio — given at a special conference of elders
Manchester, New York — spoken by Moroni during his visit to Joseph Smith
Harmony, Pennsylvania — after loss of 116 manuscript pages
Harmony, Pennsylvania — revelation to Joseph Smith Sr.
Harmony, Pennsylvania — regarding Martin Harris and the Three Witnesses
Harmony, Pennsylvania — revelation to Oliver Cowdery upon his arrival
Harmony, Pennsylvania — translated from a parchment by Joseph and Oliver
Harmony, Pennsylvania — during the translation of the Book of Mormon
Harmony, Pennsylvania — after Oliver's failed attempt to translate
Harmony, Pennsylvania — the Lord's solution to the lost manuscript pages
Harmony, Pennsylvania — revelation to Hyrum Smith
Harmony, Pennsylvania — revelation to Joseph Knight Sr.
Along the Susquehanna River near Harmony, Pennsylvania
Fayette, New York — at the Peter Whitmer Sr. farm
Fayette, New York — at the Peter Whitmer Sr. farm
Fayette, New York — at the Peter Whitmer Sr. farm
Fayette, New York — prior to the Three Witnesses experience
Fayette, New York — revelation on the organization of the Church
Manchester, New York — commandment to Martin Harris regarding the Book of Mormon printing
Fayette, New York — the day the Church of Christ was formally organized

verses.reason: One of the most cited D&C verses: the Lord's voice and His servants' voice are the same.
Command to 'search these commandments'—often used to encourage personal scripture study.
Declares the restored church as 'the only true and living church upon the face of the whole earth.'
The promise of Elijah's return to restore priesthood keys—foundational to LDS temple work.
Hearts of children turning to fathers—the doctrinal basis for family history and temple ordinances.
'The works, and the designs, and the purposes of God cannot be frustrated'—often quoted on divine sovereignty.
'You should not have feared man more than God'—a lasting lesson on moral courage.
The most frequently quoted missionary scripture: 'serve him with all your heart, might, mind and strength.'
'The field is white already to harvest'—iconic missionary imagery.
The list of Christlike attributes: faith, virtue, knowledge, temperance, patience, godliness, charity, humility, diligence.
Poetic imagery of the church 'coming forth out of the wilderness—clear as the moon, and fair as the sun.'
'Look unto me in every thought; doubt not, fear not'—widely memorized and shared.
'Seek not for riches but for wisdom'—a foundational principle on righteous priorities.
John the Beloved's request: 'Lord, give unto me power over death, that I may live and bring souls unto thee.'
Classic description of revelation: 'I will tell you in your mind and in your heart, by the Holy Ghost.'
'This is the spirit of revelation'—defining how God communicates with His children.
The most quoted verse on personal revelation: 'you must study it out in your mind' then ask, and 'your bosom shall burn within you.'
'A stupor of thought'—the counterpart to the burning bosom, widely taught in LDS settings.
'Pray always, that you may come off conqueror'—frequently used in lessons on prayer and spiritual resilience.
Succinct definition of Christ's church: 'whosoever repenteth and cometh unto me, the same is my church.'
'Seek not to declare my word, but first seek to obtain my word'—frequently cited counsel on spiritual preparation before teaching.
Promise that the Spirit 'shall enlighten your mind' and 'fill your soul with joy.'
'No one can assist in this work except he shall be humble and full of love, having faith, hope, and charity.'
The entire verse records the restoration of the Aaronic Priesthood—one of the most significant events of the Restoration.
'Eternal life, which gift is the greatest of all the gifts of God'—foundational teaching on the highest reward.
'The thing which will be of the most worth unto you will be to declare repentance'—defining the priority of missionary service.
Same declaration as Section 15—the most valuable thing is bringing souls to Christ through declaring repentance.
Promise to the Three Witnesses that they would view the plates, sword of Laban, Urim and Thummim, and Liahona.
'Remember the worth of souls is great in the sight of God'—perhaps the most quoted D&C verse.
'If it so be that you should labor all your days... and bring, save it be one soul unto me, how great shall be your joy.'
'How great will be your joy if you should bring many souls unto me!'—completing the worth-of-souls teaching.
Christ's firsthand description of Gethsemane suffering: 'to tremble because of pain, and to bleed at every pore.'
'I, God, have suffered these things for all, that they might not suffer if they would repent.'
'Learn of me, and listen to my words; walk in the meekness of my Spirit, and you shall have peace in me.'
The sacrament bread prayer—recited weekly in every LDS sacrament meeting worldwide.
The sacrament water prayer—the companion ordinance prayer used in every LDS meeting.
Defines baptismal qualifications: broken hearts, contrite spirits, willingness to take Christ's name.
